design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect scattered 'spots' throughout each level to free caged Spot characters, navigate platforming obstacles and enemies, manage health and time limits
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource collection under time pressure with health degradation from enemy contact
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a brand mascot navigating colorful, animated worlds while collecting items and defeating enemies in classic platformer fashion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on collectible-driven level progression rather than pure platforming execution; every level requires gathering a specific quantity of items before progression. Ties gameplay directly to brand identity (7 Up logo items, bubble attacks, UNCOLA bonus stage mechanic). Humorous health bar design (Spot face grimacing and falling) prioritizes visual charm over mechanical clarity.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with continues system; player takes damage from enemy contact and projectiles
difficulty.continues_system: Continue letters spelling 'UNCOLA' hidden in bonus stages; collecting a letter allows restart at current level with score reset
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Time limits enforced per level; collection quotas increase as game progresses; bonus stage access gated by successful level completion with sufficient spot collection

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level-gated progression; must collect required number of 'spots' in each level to rescue caged characters and advance
replayability_hooks: Hidden continue letters in bonus stages spelling UNCOLA encourages bonus stage replay
Score tracking incentivizes replaying levels for higher scores
Level select (debug feature suggests developer intent for replayability)

world_building_techniques: Brand integration: Spot character as 7 Up mascot with thematic visual elements
Rescue narrative: Freeing other Spot characters from cages provides light plot progression
Regional narrative adaptation: European version reframes story with generic bottle and S.O.S. note instead of 7 Up branding; Japanese version uses fictional 'TOP' brand

faction_indicators: Virgin Games branding (European version substitutes V logo for 7 Up)
danger_signals: Enemies throughout levels that deal damage on contact
Time limit pressure in each level
Increasing collection quotas signal escalating difficulty
environmental_storytelling: Caged Spot characters awaiting rescue establish mission objectives
Collectible 'spots' scattered throughout environment guide exploration
Bonus stage access tied to performance-based reward (encourage mastery)
Health bar Spot face design communicates damage state through expressive animation

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Cool Spot (SNES)
